description_long: This is a traditional stone water well with a circular stone base and a pitched, shingled roof supported by sturdy wooden beams. The well features a wooden bucket attached to a rope, which is wound around a central spindle operated by a hand crank mechanism. The stonework is robust and slightly weathered, giving the well an aged, authentic appearance. The roof is made of overlapping grayish-brown shingles, providing shelter to the well opening and the bucket mechanism. The wooden support beams are thick and arranged in a triangular truss, adding structural stability and a rustic aesthetic. The well is open at the top, with the bucket resting on the stone rim, and there are no visible decorative elements or additional barriers around the structure.
description: A classic stone water well with a shingled pitched roof, wooden support beams, and a hand-cranked bucket mechanism. The well has a weathered, rustic appearance and an open circular stone base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the shingled roof, stone base, and the bucket positioned at the well opening.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the triangular wooden truss structure and the bucket resting on the stone rim.
description_view_2: Rear view with the shingled roof and stone base visible, but the bucket and crank mechanism are less prominent.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wooden truss and bucket, similar to view 1 but from the other side.
attribute_presence_of_pulley_or_bucket_m: A hand-cranked spindle with a rope and wooden bucket is present at the center of the well.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: The well is surrounded by a circular stone wall approximately waist-high, with a pitched shingled roof supported by wooden beams.
